publications.title: Broadening alloselectivity of T cell receptors by structure guided engineering.
publications.authors: Karuppiah V, Sangani D, Whaley L, Pengelly R, Uluocak P, Carreira RJ, Hock M, Cristina PD, Bartasun P, Dobrinic P, Smith N, Barnbrook K, Robinson RA, Harper S.
publications.executive_summary: Two naturally occurring TCRs (S2 and S8) that recognize the PRAME peptide (ELFSYLIEK) presented by HLA-A*03:01 or HLA-A*11:01 were engineered to broaden their recognition across both HLA alleles. Through structure-guided affinity maturation targeting specific HLA surface residues, the researchers modulated TCR affinity to achieve comparable binding and functional potency to the same peptide on both HLA variants. This work demonstrates that TCR-based therapeutics can be rationally designed for alloselectivity—simultaneous recognition of a single antigen across multiple HLA alleles—which is critical for expanding patient eligibility in TCR immunotherapy beyond single-allele constraints and improving access across genetically diverse global populations.
